channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Valor Mortis SGF 2026 Hands-On Preview: A Genuine GOTY Contender
item.content:encoded:
Summer Games Fest 2026’s Play Days left me with the same feeling I had after leaving Gamescom 2026: Valor Mortis is a special game.

I’m going to reiterate the overriding thought I had last year: It astounds me that no one is trying to break the typical mould by making a fully fledged third-person Soulslike game. Everyone is still so busy chasing the golden FromSoftware formula that it’s easy to lose sight of ingenuity and originality.

Valor Mortis is far more than just its first-person niche.

Valor Mortis Impresses Again at Summer Game Fest 2026

I had a bold choice with my Valor Mortis preview at SGF 2026’s Play Days—play the same demo I played last time during my Gamescom preview, or throw myself into the deep end and play a new chapter midway through the game with new abilities and tools above my pay grade.

Obviously, I chose the challenge. Oh boy, was it a step up. There’s just something totally radical about a first-person view that enhances an already high level of tension.

Souls games are already tough enough, but if you’ve played any first-person game before, you know it introduces a new claustrophobic feeling by having your peripheral vision get slashed, compared to third-person.

This new chapter has a new imperious ‘elite’ enemy that takes a LOT of force to take down (You can see the big boy himself in the article’s main image). The difficulty is great, and I’m reminded once again why stamina and decision-making are paramount in Valor Mortis. Get too aggressive, and you’re vulnerable to rapid combos, and your health crumbles faster than a poorly made dessert.

You’re also highly encouraged to learn enemy weaknesses and use everything at your disposal. Enemies attack frequently and can tank a lot. You need to meticulously assess every situation like it’s life or death—which is kind of is. One enemy has an explosive barrel on its back, and if you’re patient, you can wait for its unblockable attack to pounce and fire off a Pistol round at the barrel. This deals huge damage and is an example of how Valor Mortis reinforces risk, reward, and street smarts in all facets of its gameplay.

New Chapter, Same Fun

Alongside a new hulking brute that took great pleasure in slicing up my character’s insides, the new chapter adds new weapons and new traversal abilities. Obviously, to see and do everything would have required more time, but there were more paths and even more untold dangers just lurking in every direction.

One More Level’s world-building skills are evident again, showing why Ghostrunner 1 and 2 were no fluke.

I also found the enemy placement and ‘Souls’ tricks to be well executed. There I was, decimating like Arnie in Terminator 2, only to fall painfully short before I unlocked a shortcut. One resurrection later, and my haste and impatience saw me die immediately to one of the most insignificant threats in the demo—negligence on my part, but a warning that you need to be locked in with Valor Mortis.

The heft and weightiness of the combat is unique, and I love it. Every single one of your sword attacks has a proper punch, and the swift Rapier (a new weapon in this demo) is more nimble, but comes at a great cost—You can’t block, but you do lots of damage. With my time running out, I managed to reach the boss, and there’s a grounded approach to bosses that makes them enjoyable.

Of the two I’ve faced so far, they haven’t battered me with hugely, staggered and delayed wind-up attacks, nor have they felt unfair.

Unless you’ve played Valor Mortis, it’s hard to describe how good this game is. I cannot wait for its full release and what other surprises it has in store. Can it hold up for the entire duration? I can’t tell you, but what I can tell you is that if it does, this will be in GOTY conversations when it arrives in 2026.

Did you play Valor Mortis’ playtest? Can you attest to its potential? Let me know what you think by messaging the Insider Gaming Discord.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Castlevania: Belmont’s Curse Gets October 2026 Release Date
item.content:encoded:
Castlevania: Belmont’s Curse is coming in October of this year.

First revealed during the February 2026 Sony PlayStation State of Play presentation, Castlevania: Belmont’s Curse was one of the first titles announced by Konami to celebrate the franchise’s 40th anniversary. The original Castlevania game debuted in 1986 for the Famicom Disk System and was released in America on the NES in 1987.

When revealed, it was noted that the game would be coming to PC as well as the other major consoles. Now, XBOX has revealed the game’s release date; it’s coming just in time for Halloween.

Castlevania: Belmont’s Curse Releases in Mid-October 2026

During the June 2026 XBOX Showcase, another trailer for the forthcoming game was shown, and it was revealed that it will launch on October 15. The upcoming game takes place in Paris in 1499 and sees players controlling Rose Belmont, the successor to Trevor Belmont and the last of the bloodline. She promises to hunt at night in the trailer’s final line, before the release date is shown.

The new game is also set 23 years after Castlevania: Dracula’s Curse. Dracula’s Curse was the third game in the franchise, released in 1989 for the Nintendo Entertainment System.

Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the development of Belmont’s Curse and report any updates as more information becomes available.

Check out everything announced during the XBOX Showcase here. Sound off about this story and all others on our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Clockwork Revolution Release Window & XBOX Exclusivity Revealed During XBOX Games Showcase
item.content:encoded:
New footage was shown for Clockwork Revolution at the XBOX Games Showcase, as well as a release window of 2027. As if that wasn’t enough, it’s set to be an XBOX console exclusive, alongside Gears of War: E-Day.

It’s not much of a surprise to see Clockwork Revolution during the event, thanks to an update made to a Steam banner for the Showcase, but the release date information and new look at gameplay is certainly cause for excitement.

More Clockwork Revolution Details

The breaking news for Clockwork Revolution was undoubtedly the fact that it’s due to be an XBOX exclusive, though it’s a shame it won’t see the light of day until 2027.

However, to tide us over, a huge chunk of new gameplay was revealed in a brand new trailer, showcasing the exciting time-bending, first-person action set in a uniquely steampunk setting.

Check out the brand new trailer for yourself below:

This was just one of the many announcements during the XBOX Games Showcase. If you’re looking to stay up to date on everything else, check out our live updates for the showcase here.

What do you make of Clockwork Revolution? Does its blend of Dishonoured and Bioshock action appeal to you? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about Marvel 1943: Rise of Hydra not arriving in 2026, as well as everything that was announced during Summer Game Fest.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: New Spyro the Dragon Game Announced at XBOX Showcase
item.content:encoded:
Spyro the Dragon is back. As announced during the 2026 XBOX June Showcase Event, everyone’s favorite purple dragon is returning for another adventure.

Called “A Realm Beyond,” the game was teased during the Showcase and will be the dragon’s first new Adventure on a console since 2008’s The Legend of Spyro: Dawn of the Dragon. The last overall Spyro game released was the 2018 Reignited Trilogy, which included remakes of all three original Spyro games for the Sony PlayStation.

The new game launches in 2027 for all major consoles and PC.

Spyro The Dragon’s Past As a Pseudo PlayStation Mascot and Handheld Icon

In the late 90s and early 2000s, Spyro was viewed as somewhat of a PlayStation mascot alongside Crash Bandicoot, Lara Croft, and PaRappa the Rapper. Through the years, Spyro would be featured on Nintendo handheld consoles alongside the aforementioned Bandicoot.

Crash got his own new adventure in 2020 with Crash Bandicoot 4: It’s About Time. That game was also developed by Toys for Bob, the same company developing the new Spyro game.

Insider Gaming will continue to monitor all things Spyro the Dragon and report any updates as they are made available. You know who might be stoked for a new Spyro the Dragon game? Current WWE Women’s Champion Rhea Ripley. She recently named the dragon one of her favorite gaming IPs from her childhood. Learn more here.

Check out everything announced during the XBOX Showcase here. Sound off about this story and all others on our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

Doom: The Dark Ages Revelations DLC Release Date Revealed

id Software has revealed a brand new DLC for Doom the Dark Ages, with Revelations releasing this Summer. Here are more details.

Minecraft Dungeons 2 Set for September 2026 Release

Minecraft Dungeons 2 received a new look during the recent XBOX Showcase in June 2026.

Valor Mortis Announced for Xbox Game Pass, Releases September 2026

item.title: Persona 6 Announced During XBOX Games Showcase
item.content:encoded:
After more than a decade since the last mainline outing for the franchise, Persona 6 has officially been unveiled during the 2026 XBOX Games Showcase.

Atlus has been tiding fans over with remakes of Persona 3 and Persona 4, with Persona 4 Revival currently in development. Thankfully, it’s looking like it’s time for a new story altogether, and judging by the trailer, it’s bound to be as stylish and slick as ever before.

More Persona 6 Details

As you might expect, the reveal trailer was incredibly Persona, though it was admittedly quite gothic. Graves everywhere, and a slightly horror-esque flashing of images closed it out, before we got a look at the new color theme of Persona 6: neon green.

No details of a release date were in sight, and with Persona 4 Revival due early 2027, it’s unlikely we’ll see Persona 6 until late 2027 at the earliest—though just the announcement is exciting enough!

Check out the brand new trailer for yourself below:

This was just one of the many announcements during the XBOX Games Showcase. If you’re looking to stay up to date on everything else, check out our live updates for the showcase here.

If you’re anything like me, you must be incredibly hyped to see a new mainline entry into the Persona franchise, but let your thoughts be heard in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about Marvel 1943: Rise of Hydra not arriving in 2026, as well as everything that was announced during Summer Game Fest.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: Minecraft Dungeons 2 Set for September 2026 Release
item.content:encoded:
Minecraft Dungeons 2 received a new look during the recent XBOX Showcase in June 2026.

The forthcoming Minecraft Dungeons sequel was officially announced in March of this year. Now, it has received a new look during the recent Xbox Showcase. The game will be coming out in September 2026. The September release date was previously leaked due to an accidental early posting on the Nintendo eShop.

Now, it’s been confirmed that the game will release on September 29. It will also be playable on XBOX Game Pass day one. Many of the games showcased on June 7 have also had that perk announced.

More Recent Minecraft News

The previous Nintendo League also noted that the forthcoming Dungeons game is priced at $29.99 USD; a host of other details have been made available, including an estimated game file size of 6.8GB and both local and online four-player multiplayer—as expected based on the original. Multiplayer was heavily featured in the trailer as part of the XBOX showcase.

In other Minecraft news, a sequel to the movie starring Jack Black was recently announced. There’s also a creator contest in conjunction with the movie that will allow talented creators in the Minecraft world to potentially see some of their Creations come to life on the big screen. Learn more about that here.

Check out everything announced during the XBOX Showcase here. Sound off about this story and all others on our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

Sega has revealed its long-awaited Crazy Taxi reboot, introducing the franchise’s first major console entry since 2002.

Xbox and Ninja Theory have announced Senua at the Xbox Showcase.
